 25.       └─rlang::abort(msg, call = call)   

⏺ Yes, the CRS warning during st_transform is harmless — it's just
  informational.
                                                                               
  The DuckDB error is because the wrangling database uses an older storage     
  format (v1.0.0) that doesn't support CRS-tagged geometry columns. The        
  geoparquet files embed CRS metadata which DuckDB tries to preserve. The fix  
  is to strip the CRS when loading into DuckDB.  

⏺ Update(ingest_spatial.qmd)

⏺ This fix reads the parquet into a temp view, detects the geometry column,
  then round-trips it through ST_AsWKB() → ST_GeomFromWKB() which strips the
  CRS identifier. The resulting GEOMETRY column is CRS-free and compatible with
   the older DuckDB storage format.

⏺ The CORS file format is wrong for the gcloud storage command. It expects a
  JSON array at the top level, not an object with a "cors" key. Also, PMTiles
  needs Range header support for byte-range requests.                          
   
  Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)                                               
                                                                  
⏺ Write(~/Github/CalCOFI/int-app/data/_cors_file.json)
  ⎿  Added 14 lines, removed 10 lines                 
       1 -{                                                                    
       2 - "cors": [                                                           
       3 -    {                                                                
       4 -        "origin": ["*"],                                             
       5 -        "method": ["GET"],                                           
       6 -        "responseHeader": ["Content-Type"],                          
       7 -        "maxAgeSeconds": 1800                                        
       8 -      } 
       9 -  ] 
      10 -} 
       1 +[
       2 +  {
       3 +    "origin": ["*"],
       4 +    "method": ["GET", "HEAD"],
       5 +    "responseHeader": [
       6 +      "Content-Type",
       7 +      "Content-Range",
       8 +      "Accept-Ranges",
       9 +      "Content-Length",
      10 +      "Range" 
      11 +    ],
      12 +    "maxAgeSeconds": 3600 
      13 +  }
      14 +]

⏺ Two issues with the original file:

  1. Wrong JSON structure — gcloud storage buckets update --cors-file expects a
   top-level array [{...}], not {"cors": [{...}]} (the {"cors":...} wrapper was
   for the older gsutil cors set command)                                      
  2. Missing headers for PMTiles — PMTiles uses HTTP range requests, so
  Content-Range, Accept-Ranges, and Range must be in responseHeader. Also added
   HEAD method.
                                                                               
  Now re-run the command:                                                      
   
  cd ~/Github/CalCOFI/int-app/data                                             
  gcloud storage buckets update gs://calcofi-files-public         
  --cors-file=_cors_file.json

  You can verify it worked with:                                               
   
  gcloud storage buckets describe gs://calcofi-files-public --format="json(cors_config)"

⏺ Now add back promote_id on the source and hover_options on all layers:

⏺ Here's a summary of all changes:   

  `ingest_spatial.qmd` — Added --use-attribute-for-id=id to the tippecanoe       
  command. This promotes the id column (which we ensure exists as an integer in
   every dataset) as the vector tile feature ID. This is what MapLibre's       
  setFeatureState needs to track hover/click state on PMTiles features.

  `functions.R` — Three changes in `add_spatial_layers()`:                         
  - Restored promote_id = "id" on `add_pmtiles_source()` — tells MapLibre to use
  the id property as feature IDs                                               
  - Restored hover_options on all PMTiles layers (line, fill, circle) with
  yellow (#ffeb3b) highlight                                                   
  - Added `popup = tt` alongside `tooltip = tt` so clicking shows the same field   
  value in a persistent popup                                               
                                                                               
  `ui.R` — Added CSS to darken tooltip/popup text: .maplibregl-popup-content { 
  color: #1a1a1a; font-weight: 500; }                                          
                                                                  
  To apply: You'll need to re-run `ingest_spatial.qmd` with `redo_if_exists <- TRUE` 
  to regenerate all PMTiles with proper feature IDs, then re-upload to
  GCS. The existing PMTiles were generated without `--use-attribute-for-id=id`,  
  so they lack the feature IDs that setFeatureState requires.  

## 2026-03-26.b setup erddap server

Update docker-compose.yml and this server repo to include an ERDDAP server   
instance, per https://hub.docker.com/r/erddap/erddap  

Also see https://ioos.github.io/erddap-gold-standard/index.html 

⏺ Here's a summary of the changes:
                                                                               
  Files created                                                   
                                                                               
  - `erddap/content/setup.xml` -- ERDDAP server configuration with CalCOFI admin 
  info (institution, La Jolla/CA, calcofi@ucsd.edu). Most values can be
  overridden by ERDDAP_* env vars in docker-compose.                           
  - `erddap/content/datasets.xml` -- Starter datasets file with global settings.
  Add <dataset> entries here as you onboard data.                              
  - `erddap/data/logs/` -- Directory for ERDDAP runtime logs/cache.
                                                                               
  Files modified                                                  
                                                                               
  - `docker-compose.yml` -- Added erddap_data named volume and erddap service:   
    - Image: erddap/erddap:latest (official Docker Hub image)
    - Port: 8090:8080 (8080 already used by pg_swagger)                        
    - 4 volume mounts: config content, persistent data, /share, and            
  /share/erddap_datasets → /datasets for source data files                     
    - CalCOFI-specific env vars for admin metadata                             
    - 4GB JVM heap (ERDDAP_MEMORY)                                             
  - `caddy/Caddyfile` -- Added erddap.calcofi.io reverse proxy → erddap:8080     
                                                                               
  Next steps on the server                                                     
                                                                               
  1. Create the datasets directory: `sudo mkdir -p /share/erddap_datasets`
  2. Set `ERDDAP_flagKeyKey` -- Add a unique random string to the environment
  block in docker-compose.yml (security key for remote dataset reloading)      
  3. Add datasets -- Place data files (NetCDF, CSV) in /share/erddap_datasets/
  and add corresponding <dataset> entries to `erddap/content/datasets.xml`. You  
  can use ERDDAP's `GenerateDatasetsXml` tool to auto-generate XML snippets.
  4. DNS -- Point `erddap.calcofi.io` to the server (Caddy will auto-provision   
  the TLS certificate)                                                         
  5. Deploy: `docker compose up -d erddap` then restart caddy: `docker compose 
  restart caddy`

  ┌───────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┬──────────────┐
  │      Server path      │        Container mount         │   Purpose    │
  ├───────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────┼──────────────┤

  ├───────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────┼──────────────┤

  │ /share/erddap/dataset │ /datasets                      │ files        │
  │ s/                    │                                │ (NetCDF,     │
  │                       │                                │ CSV, etc.)   │
  └───────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────┴──────────────┘

  Server setup before first run

  sudo mkdir -p /share/erddap/{content,data/logs,datasets}

  Then create setup.xml and datasets.xml in /share/erddap/content/ on the
  server (following the https://ioos.github.io/erddap-gold-standard/index.html
  templates). Nothing from the erddap/ directory is tracked in this repo
  anymore.

⏺ Yes, that's a good pattern -- a separate repo for ERDDAP config that gets
  cloned to /share/erddap on the server. Something like CalCOFI/erddap with:

  erddap/
    content/
      setup.xml
      datasets.xml
      images/
    .gitignore       # ignore data/ (logs, cache, flags)

  The data/ and datasets/ directories would be .gitignored since they contain
  runtime state and large data files, respectively.

  Want me to create that repo structure locally so you can push it to
  CalCOFI/erddap?

⏺ Here's the final layout:

  CalCOFI/erddap repo (new, tracked in git):
  content/
    setup.xml        # server config
    datasets.xml     # dataset definitions
    images/          # custom branding
  .gitignore         # ignores data/ and datasets/

  On server at /share (not in git):
  /share/github/CalCOFI/erddap/   # git clone of the repo above
  /share/erddap/data/              # runtime logs, cache, flags
  /share/erddap/datasets/          # source data files (NetCDF, CSV)

  Server setup
  ```r
  # clone config repo
  cd /share/github/CalCOFI
  git clone https://github.com/CalCOFI/erddap.git

  # create runtime dirs
  sudo mkdir -p /share/erddap/{data/logs,datasets}

  You'll want to create the CalCOFI/erddap repo on GitHub, then push from
  /Users/bbest/Github/CalCOFI/erddap. Want me to create it with gh repo create?
  ```

## 2026-03-26 + calcofi4r::cc_erd()

The database schema visualization using `dm::dm_draw()` and its antecedent functions cannot handle geometry columns, which causes problems in showing critical tables (`segment`, `casts`, `grid`, `site`, `dic_sample`, `ctd_cast`). This has even led in `@release_database.qmd` to erroneously excluding these tables in the final frozen release database.

Create a new function in `@../calcofi4r/` that reads the database, extracts table and column information along with relationships and generates an entity relationship diagram (ERD) in mermaid format with options for color coding (like current dm_draw() implementation). See - [Entity Relationship Diagrams | Mermaid](https://mermaid.ai/open-source/syntax/entityRelationshipDiagram.html).


I meant create a new function in `@../calcofi4r/` (not `@../msens/`). 

Here also is some useful DuckDB SQL (I was unable to install the required `webmacro` to get this to work):
- [mermaid.sql](https://gist.github.com/lmangani/dc9ea2ba0a0b2a54a1330e7db868e0bc)


Per your suggestion:

> `knit_print.cc_erd(x, ...)` - renders via DiagrammeR + color JS injection

The `DiagrammeR` R package does not seem to support ERD mermaid diagrams, but Quarto does. See [IMAGE #]. The core function should generate the mermaid ERD syntax, which could be passed into a Quarto doc directly or as an include (which renders natively on Github) and already we have a folder `@diagrams/`. Let's also consider using the [Mermaid client](https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid-cli) for rendering to PNG, such as for display in a Shiny app.

⏺ The plan creates cc_erd() in calcofi4r/R/erd.R that generates Mermaid ERDs by
   querying information_schema.columns (which handles GEOMETRY fine), reads    
  PK/FK from relationships.json, and supports color-coded table groups. It     
  renders automatically in Quarto via DiagrammeR::mermaid() with JS-based color
   injection.                                                                  

 Ready to code?
                                                                               
 Here is Claude's plan:

 Plan: cc_erd() function for calcofi4r                  

 Context

 dm::dm_draw() fails on DuckDB GEOMETRY columns, forcing spatial tables (site,
  grid, casts, segment, ctd_cast, dic_sample) to be excluded from ERD
 diagrams. This has also caused these tables to be erroneously omitted from
 the frozen release database. The fix: bypass dm entirely for ERD generation,
 using information_schema.columns which reports GEOMETRY as a plain string
 type.

 Files to create/modify

 ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────┐
 │                       File                        │       Action        │
 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
 │                                                   │ Create - new file   │
 │ /Users/bbest/Github/CalCOFI/calcofi4r/R/erd.R     │ with cc_erd() and   │
 │                                                   │ helpers             │
 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
 │                                                   │ Edit - add          │
 │ /Users/bbest/Github/CalCOFI/calcofi4r/DESCRIPTION │ DiagrammeR,         │
 │                                                   │ htmlwidgets to      │
 │                                                   │ Suggests            │
 └───────────────────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────┘

 Implementation

 1. Create R/erd.R

 Exported functions:
 - cc_erd(con, tables, exclude, rels_path, colors, rankdir, view_type) - main
 function
 - print.cc_erd(x, ...) - outputs raw Mermaid code via cat()
 - knit_print.cc_erd(x, ...) - renders via DiagrammeR + color JS injection

 Internal helpers:
 - .erd_shorten_type(dtype) - DuckDB types to compact labels (e.g., INTEGER ->
  int, GEOMETRY(POINT) -> geometry)
 - .erd_color_to_hex(color) - R color names to hex via grDevices::col2rgb()
 - .erd_build_color_map(colors) - named list -> table->hex lookup
 - .erd_build_color_js(color_map) - JS for htmlwidgets::onRender() to fill
 entity rects

 Key design decisions:
 - Returns structure(mermaid_code, class = c("cc_erd", "character"), colors =
 colors, tables = tables)
 - rels_path is optional; when NULL, diagram shows tables without relationship
  lines
 - colors is a named list: list(lightblue = c("cruise", "ship"), pink =
 c("casts")) - matches dm::dm_set_colors() convention
 - Uses %%{init: {'er': {'layoutDirection': 'LR'}}}%% for Mermaid layout
 direction
 - Cardinality: all FKs rendered as ||--o{ (one-to-many, standard FK pattern)
 - view_type: "all" (all columns), "keys_only" (PK/FK only), "title_only" (no
 columns)

 Schema extraction SQL:
 SELECT table_name, column_name, data_type, ordinal_position
 FROM information_schema.columns
 WHERE table_name IN (...)
 ORDER BY table_name, ordinal_position

 Mermaid output format:
 %%{init: {'er': {'layoutDirection': 'LR'}}}%%
 erDiagram
     casts {
         int cast_id PK
         varchar cruise_key FK
         geometry geom
     }
     cruise ||--o{ casts : "cruise_key"

 knit_print color injection: Uses htmlwidgets::onRender() with JS that selects
  .er.entityBox rects inside g[id^="entity-"] elements, maps entity names to
 hex colors from the color_map attribute. Uses setTimeout to wait for Mermaid
 SVG rendering.

 2. Update DESCRIPTION

 Add to existing Suggests section:
 Suggests:
     DiagrammeR,
     htmlwidgets,
     knitr,
     rmarkdown

 3. Run devtools::document()

 Regenerates NAMESPACE with:
 - export(cc_erd)
 - S3method(print,cc_erd)
 - S3method(knitr::knit_print,cc_erd)

 information_schema.columns for GEOMETRY detection

 @importFrom glue glue

 PK/FK parsing pattern

## 2026-03-24 ∆ cruise_key, match tracks, track stats in release_database.qmd

Come up with a plan to develop a `../apps/cruises` R Shiny app that shows:

- **Table** of `cruise_key` by row sorted by year-month, and columns for each dataset (skip provider prefix for brevity) with the number of unique sites (xyt) included. Add a _**Map**_ button to view the given cruise.

- **Map** of the given cruise enabling interactive exploration, to show all the sites, grids, and segments by dataset. Use the `mapgl` library

Development of this app should look to and improve upon the existing `ingest_{dataset}_{provider}.qmd` notebooks:

- `@ingest_swfsc_ichthyo.qmd`
- `@ingest_calcofi_bottle.qmd`
- `@ingest_calcofi_ctd-cast.qmd`
- `@ingest_calcofi_dic.qmd`
- `@release_database.qmd`
- `@publish_ichthyo_to_obis.qmd`

 Also update associated `.claude/skills` along with R packages `@../calcofi4db` for internal db ingestion and `@../calcofi4r` for external integrated database usage with the following goals:

- **Cruise**
  - `cruise_key`: There is disagreement across datasets about this fundamental identifier. The `ingest_calcofi_ctd-cast.qmd` uses `YYMMKK` (`YY`: 2 digit year; `MM`: 2 digit month; `KK`: 2 letter shp id), like "9802JD", however this does not sort well by date. Let's instead use the less ambiguous `YYYY-MM-NODC` (`YYYY`: 4 digit year; `MM`: 2 digit month; `NODC`: 4 character NODC ship code) across datasets.
  - `ship` table: per dataset, we should see which rows match, and which are new or mismatched after using `calcofi4db::match_ships()`.

- **Temporal**
  - `datetime_utc`: This should be the standard column for time and in UTC.

- **Spatial**
  - `line`, `station`: what decimal precision is in use across datasets and should be applied here in a standard way?
  - `site_key`: `{line station}`
  - `grid_key`: This should be the standard column for grid identifiers.
  - `segment` table: Should this be populated per dataset and then merged? Ideally some match, like `bottle` and `dic`.

- Report on alignment across datasets for these in `release_database.qmd`.

Also see:

- [ingest: spatial matching for DIC and future datasets (site, grid, segment) · Issue #47 · CalCOFI/workflows](https://github.com/CalCOFI/workflows/issues/47)

←  ☐ NODC code  ☐ site_key  ☐ Cruise tracks  ☐ Precision  ✔ Submit  → 

**NODC code**. For the new cruise_key format YYYY-MM-NODC: the ship table has `ship_key` (2-letter, e.g., 'JD') and 
`ship_nodc` (4-char NODC code, e.g., '33JD'). The current YYMMKK uses ship_key. Should the new format 
use `ship_nodc` (e.g., '1998-02-33JD') or `ship_key` (e.g., '1998-02-JD')?

❯ 1. ship_nodc (Recommended)      ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
  2. ship_key (2-letter)          │ cruise_key examples with ship_nodc:      │
                                  │   1998-02-33JD   (R/V Jordan)            │
                                  │   2015-07-325S   (R/V Shimada)           │
                                  │   2024-01-33UD   (R/V Lasker)            │
                                  │   1949-03-33CR   (early CalCOFI)         │
                                  └──────────────────────────────────────────┘

**`site_key`**. Should `site_key` replace `sta_key` everywhere (database tables, calcofi4r bundled data, skills), or 
coexist as an alias?                                                                                  
                                                                          
  1. Replace sta_key (Recommended)
     Rename sta_key → site_key in all DB tables, ingest notebooks, and skills. Add deprecation alias 
     in calcofi4r's cc_grid for one release cycle. Clean break.               
  2. Coexist as alias                                                         
     Keep sta_key in existing tables, add site_key as computed alias. Avoids breaking downstream code 
     but adds confusion about which to use.                                   
❯ 3. Looking at `@../calcofi4r/data-raw/cc_grid.R#L252`, I see:
  ```r
  V <-  V %>%
    mutate(
      sta_key = glue("{lin},{pos}")) %>%
    relocate(sta_key)
  ```
  The problem is that I previously updated the "station" to "position" in `calcofi4r` library, but "line" and "station" are historically too fully baked into the CalCOFI projection system to change that. Instead, I am now deciding to change the combination of "station & position" from the redundant "station" to the new term "site". Let's update `cc_grid` in `calcofi4r` and be consistent throughout the dataset ingestion to use `site_key` (to replace `sta_key`).

**Cruise track**. For the Shiny app's Map view of a cruise: how should cruise 'tracks' be shown for non-ichthyo datasets
 (bottle, ctd, dic) which are point observations, not towed transects?                                
                                                                          
❯ 1. Connect sequential casts     
     Draw lines between casts ordered by datetime_utc within a cruise — visual cruise track, computed 
     on-the-fly in the app (like ctd-viz app's compute_segments). No new DB table needed.
  2. Points only, no lines                                                    
     Show cast positions as colored points per dataset, with grid polygons. No connecting lines for   
     non-ichthyo. Simpler and avoids implying a tow path that didn't happen.  
  3. Both options, togglable                                                  
     Show points by default, with a toggle to optionally display connecting lines as an approximate   
     cruise track. Most flexible but more UI complexity.

**Precision**. What should the line/station decimal precision standard be? Currently bottle/ctd/dic use 1 decimal    
(e.g., '090.0 062.0'), while ichthyo site table has raw doubles. Should we standardize to 1 decimal   
everywhere?                                                               
                                  
❯ 1. 1 decimal (Recommended)                                                                          
     Standardize to 1 decimal place: '090.0 062.0'. Matches existing bottle/ctd/dic convention. 
     Ichthyo site values would be formatted to match (e.g., line=90 → '090.0').
  2. Variable precision                                                                               
     Keep raw numeric precision per dataset. site_key would use whatever precision the source 
     provides. Risk: '90.0 62.0' vs '090.0 062.0' mismatches across datasets.


⏺ Updated plan
  ⎿  /plan to preview

 Ready to code?

 Here is Claude's plan:

 Plan: Cruises Shiny App + Identifier Standardization

 Context

 CalCOFI's four datasets (ichthyo, bottle, ctd-cast, dic) use inconsistent
 identifiers for cruises, ships, sites, and spatial grids, making
 cross-dataset queries fragile. The cruise_key format YYMMKK doesn't sort
 chronologically and uses internal 2-letter ship codes. Station identifiers
 (sta_key) vary in format across datasets and the calcofi4r package. A Shiny
 app is needed to visualize cruise coverage across all datasets.

 Goals:
 1. Standardize cruise_key to YYYY-MM-{ship_nodc} (e.g., 1998-02-33JD)
 2. Rename sta_key → site_key everywhere, with consistent NNN.N NNN.N format
 3. Build a ../apps/cruises Shiny app with Table + Map views
 4. Update calcofi4db, calcofi4r, ingest notebooks, and skills

 ---
 Design Decisions (confirmed with user)

 ┌─────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
 │      Decision       │                      Choice                      │
 ├─────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ cruise_key NODC     │ ship_nodc (4-char, e.g., "33JD", "325S")         │
 │ component           │                                                  │
 ├─────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ site_key vs sta_key │ Replace sta_key everywhere; update cc_grid in    │
 │                     │ calcofi4r                                        │
 ├─────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ Cruise tracks on    │ Connect sequential casts by datetime (like       │
 │ map                 │ ctd-viz compute_segments())                      │
 ├─────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ Line/station        │ 1 decimal everywhere: PRINTF('%05.1f %05.1f',    │
 │ precision           │ line, station)                                   │
 └─────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 ---
 Phase 1: calcofi4db package changes

 1A. Update `create_cruise_key()` → `YYYY-MM-NODC`

 File: /Users/bbest/Github/CalCOFI/calcofi4db/R/wrangle.R (lines 27-65)

 Change the SQL from YYMMKK to YYYY-MM-NODC. Current code builds
 LPAD(YEAR%100,2) || LPAD(MONTH,2) || ship_key. New code JOINs to ship table
 for ship_nodc:

 ```sql
 UPDATE {cruise_tbl} AS cr
 SET cruise_key = CONCAT(
   CAST(EXTRACT(YEAR FROM cr.{date_col}) AS VARCHAR),
   '-',
   LPAD(CAST(EXTRACT(MONTH FROM cr.{date_col}) AS VARCHAR), 2, '0'),
   '-',
   s.ship_nodc)
 FROM {ship_tbl} s
 WHERE cr.ship_key = s.ship_key
 ```

 Update docstring from "YYMMKK" to "YYYY-MM-NODC". Update @param docs.

 1B. Update `derive_cruise_key_on_casts()` → `YYYY-MM-NODC`

 File: /Users/bbest/Github/CalCOFI/calcofi4db/R/ship.R (lines 491-499)

 Change the cruise_key derivation SQL at line 494-498:

 ```sql
 UPDATE casts SET cruise_key = CONCAT(
   CAST(EXTRACT(YEAR FROM datetime_utc) AS VARCHAR),
   '-',
   LPAD(CAST(EXTRACT(MONTH FROM datetime_utc) AS VARCHAR), 2, '0'),
   '-',
   (SELECT s.ship_nodc FROM ship s WHERE s.ship_key = casts.ship_key LIMIT 1))
 WHERE ship_key IS NOT NULL
 ```

 Update docstring. The validation query at lines 506-518 works unchanged (just
  compares cruise_key strings).

 1C. Add convert_cruise_key_format() helper

 File: /Users/bbest/Github/CalCOFI/calcofi4db/R/wrangle.R (new function)

 For CTD filenames that embed old YYMMKK format. Needs ship table for KK→NODC
 mapping:

 ```r
 convert_cruise_key_format <- function(con, old_key_col, table, ship_tbl =
 "ship") {
   # century rule: YY >= 49 → 19YY, YY < 49 → 20YY (CalCOFI starts 1949)
   # extract ship_key = RIGHT(old_key, 2), look up ship_nodc
   # build YYYY-MM-NODC
 }
 ```

 1D. Add standardize_site_key() helper

 File: /Users/bbest/Github/CalCOFI/calcofi4db/R/wrangle.R (new function)

 ```r
 standardize_site_key <- function(con, table, line_col = "line", station_col =
  "station") {
   # adds site_key column as PRINTF('%05.1f %05.1f', line, station)
   # validates 1-decimal precision
 }
 ```

 DuckDB SQL: `PRINTF('%05.1f %05.1f', {line_col}, {station_col})`

 Examples: line=90.0, station=62.0 → "090.0 062.0"; line=93.3, station=110.0 →
  "093.3 110.0"

 1E. Add report_ship_matches() function

 File: /Users/bbest/Github/CalCOFI/calcofi4db/R/ship.R (new function)

 Reports per-dataset ship matching status: matched/unmatched/new ships.

 1F. Run devtools::document() and rebuild

 File: /Users/bbest/Github/CalCOFI/calcofi4db/DESCRIPTION — no new deps needed

 ---
 Phase 2: calcofi4r package changes

 2A. Rename `sta_key` → `site_key` in cc_grid data

 File: /Users/bbest/Github/CalCOFI/calcofi4r/data-raw/cc_grid.R

 - Line 251: `sta_key = glue("{lin},{pos}")` → `site_key = glue("{lin},{pos}")`
   - Note: Keep {lin},{pos} comma format here since cc_grid uses CalCOFI
 projection coords directly. The DB site_key uses the 0-padded format. The
 grid table provides the bridge between the two via line/station columns.
   - Actually, to be consistent: change to `site_key = sprintf("%05.1f %05.1f",lin, pos)` to match the database format
 - Line 329: select(sta_key, sta_lin = lin, sta_pos = pos, sta_dpos = dpos) →
 rename sta_key to site_key
 - Update all sta_key references in this file

 File: /Users/bbest/Github/CalCOFI/calcofi4r/R/data.R

 change sta_key to site_key

 fine.csv

 File: /Users/bbest/Github/CalCOFI/workflows/ingest_calcofi_ctd-cast.qmd

 - Rename all sta_key → site_key (5 occurrences)
 - CTD zip filenames embed old YYMMKK format. After extracting the key from
 filenames, use convert_cruise_key_format() to convert to YYYY-MM-NODC
 - Line ~894: ship_key = RIGHT(cruise_key, 2) must change → extract NODC from
 new format: SPLIT_PART(cruise_key, '-', 3) then look up ship_key from ship
 table
 - ctd_summary references to sta_key → site_key

 3D. ingest_calcofi_dic.qmd

 File: /Users/bbest/Github/CalCOFI/workflows/ingest_calcofi_dic.qmd

 - Rename all sta_key → site_key (22 occurrences)
 - Cast matching queries: d.sta_key = c.sta_key → d.site_key = c.site_key
 - Issue #47 spatial matching: Add spatial fallback for unmatched DIC samples:
 -- find nearest cast within 3-day window by spatial distance
 UPDATE dic_sample d
 SET cast_id = (
   SELECT c.cast_id FROM casts c
   WHERE ABS(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM c.datetime_utc - d.datetime_utc)) < 259200
   ORDER BY ST_Distance(ST_Point(d.longitude, d.latitude), c.geom)
   LIMIT 1)
 WHERE d.cast_id IS NULL
   AND d.latitude IS NOT NULL AND d.longitude IS NOT NULL

 3E. release_database.qmd

 File: /Users/bbest/Github/CalCOFI/workflows/release_database.qmd

 - Update all sta_key → site_key (6 occurrences)
 - Add validation checks:
   - cruise_key format: WHERE cruise_key NOT SIMILAR TO '\d{4}-\d{2}-.+'
   - site_key format: WHERE site_key NOT SIMILAR TO '\d{3}\.\d \d{3}\.\d'
   - datetime_utc column exists and is TIMESTAMP type
 - Add a cruise_summary derived table for the Shiny app:
 CREATE TABLE cruise_summary AS
 SELECT
   cr.cruise_key,
   EXTRACT(YEAR FROM cr.date_ym) AS year,
   EXTRACT(MONTH FROM cr.date_ym) AS month,
   s.ship_name, s.ship_nodc,
   -- counts per dataset
   (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT site_key) FROM site WHERE cruise_key =
 cr.cruise_key) AS ichthyo,
   (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT site_key) FROM casts WHERE cruise_key =
 cr.cruise_key) AS bottle,
   (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT site_key) FROM ctd_cast WHERE cruise_key =
 cr.cruise_key) AS ctd_cast,
   (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT site_key) FROM dic_sample ds
      JOIN casts c ON ds.cast_id = c.cast_id
      WHERE c.cruise_key = cr.cruise_key) AS dic
 FROM cruise cr
 JOIN ship s ON cr.ship_key = s.ship_key
 ORDER BY year DESC, month DESC
 - Export cruise_summary to parquet alongside other tables
 - Update relationships.json for site_key rename

 New directory: /Users/bbest/Github/CalCOFI/apps/cruises/

 4A. global.R

 Follow ctd-viz app pattern (best existing template):

 ```r
 librarian::shelf(
   calcofi/calcofi4r,
   bslib, bsicons, DBI, dbplyr, dplyr, DT, glue,
   mapgl, purrr, sf, shiny, stringr, tibble, tidyr,
   quiet = T)

 con <- calcofi4r::cc_get_db()

 # build cruise summary from DB tables
 # query each dataset's table for COUNT(DISTINCT site_key) per cruise_key
 # join to cruise + ship tables for metadata
 # result: d_summary tibble with columns:
 #   cruise_key, year, month, ship_name, ichthyo, bottle, ctd_cast, dic

 # helper: compute_segments() — reuse pattern from ctd-viz/global.R lines
 55-76
 # connects sequential casts by datetime within a cruise

 onStop(function() { dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE) })
 ```

 Data source: calcofi4r::cc_get_db() (released DB). If cruise_summary table
 exists in release, use it directly; otherwise compute from component tables.

 4B. ui.R

 ```r
 page_fillable(
   title = "CalCOFI Cruises Explorer",
   navset_card_pill(
     id = "navset",
     placement = "above",
     nav_panel(
       title = "Table",
       icon  = bs_icon("table"),
       card(
         full_screen = TRUE,
         DTOutput("tbl_cruises"))
     ),
     nav_panel(
       title = "Map",
       icon  = bs_icon("map"),
       card(
         full_screen = TRUE,
         mapgl::maplibreOutput("map_cruise", height = "100%"))
     )
   )
 )
 ```

 dic, Map (button)

 - Color-code points by dataset: ichthyo=#ff6b6b, bottle=#4ecdc4,
 ctd-cast=#45b7d1, dic=#f9ca24
 - Show grid polygons from grid table (read via calcofi4r::cc_read_sf())
 - Show ichthyo segments from segment table if available
 - Connect sequential casts by datetime for bottle/ctd tracks (using
 compute_segments())
 - Popup on click: site_key, datetime_utc, grid_key, dataset
 - mapgl::add_legend() for dataset colors

 ---
 Implementation Order

 Phase 1: calcofi4db (1A-1F)     ← foundation, must go first
 Phase 2: calcofi4r (2A-2B)      ← cc_grid rename, can parallel with Phase 1
 Phase 3A: ichthyo notebook      ← produces ship/cruise/grid tables
 Phase 3B: bottle notebook       ← depends on 3A tables
 Phase 3C: ctd-cast notebook     ← depends on 3A tables
 Phase 3D: dic notebook          ← depends on 3B (casts table)
 Phase 3E: release_database      ← depends on all 3A-3D
 Phase 3F: publish_ichthyo_obis  ← depends on 3E
 Phase 4: Shiny app              ← can begin after Phase 2, final test after
 3E
 Phase 5: Skills                 ← independent, can parallel with anything

 Recommendation: Start with Phases 1+2+5 in parallel (package changes +
 skills). Then 3A→3B→3C→3D→3E sequentially. Then Phase 4.

   - grid_key assignment coverage unchanged
   - Ship match reports show no regressions

 populated

 Map button shows cruise sites with correct positions

⏺ All 5 notebooks rendered successfully!

  Pipeline complete:
  1. ingest_swfsc_ichthyo.qmd
  2. ingest_calcofi_bottle.qmd
  3. ingest_calcofi_ctd-cast.qmd
  4. ingest_calcofi_dic.qmd
  5. release_database.qmd

  The recurring issue throughout was DuckDB's R driver not supporting GEOMETRY
  columns in tbl(), dbListFields(), and dm_from_con(). All fixes were
  workarounds using information_schema.columns queries and ST_AsText()
  conversions. These are pre-existing DuckDB/R driver limitations, not caused
  by our identifier changes.

⏺ Validation Report: ncei_dic

  Summary

  - Status: WARN (functional, with expected limitations)
  - Tables: 3 dataset tables (dic_sample, dic_measurement, measurement_type)
  - Errors: 1 (measurement_prec 100% NULL — expected, NCEI has no precision column)
  - Warnings: 3 (see below)

  Results

  ┌────────────────────────┬────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │         Check          │ Status │                           Details                           │
  ├────────────────────────┼────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Spatial                │ PASS   │ 28.1-37.0°N, -125.1 to -117.3°W — within CalCOFI grid       │
  ├────────────────────────┼────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Temporal               │ PASS   │ 1983-03-19 to 2021-07-20                                    │
  ├────────────────────────┼────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ measurement_type FK    │ PASS   │ All types registered                                        │
  ├────────────────────────┼────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ dic_sample composite   │ WARN   │ 296 duplicate (expocode, sta_key, depth, date) — replicate  │
  │ PK                     │        │ samples at same location                                    │
  ├────────────────────────┼────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ dic_measurement        │ WARN   │ 258 duplicate (bottle_id, measurement_type) — multiple      │
  │ composite PK           │        │ DIC/TA values per bottle                                    │
  ├────────────────────────┼────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ cast_id match rate     │ WARN   │ 24.7% (1,086/4,391) — many DIC cruises pre-date or differ   │
  │                        │        │ from calcofi.org bottle coverage                            │
  ├────────────────────────┼────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ measurement_prec       │ INFO   │ 100% NULL — NCEI data uses WOCE flags, not precision values │
  └────────────────────────┴────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Key Findings

  1. Cast matching at 24.7% — The NCEI DIC data goes back to 1983 and includes cruises/stations that
  may not be in the calcofi.org bottle database (1949-2021). Many early DIC measurements were from
  cruises where the bottle data isn't in the calcofi.org download.
  2. Replicate samples — DIC and TA often have multiple measurements per bottle (replicate analyses).
  The notebook flags 258 duplicate (bottle_id, measurement_type) pairs. These need a decision: average
   replicates, or keep as separate rows with a replicate number.
  3. Parquet export successful — 5 files uploaded to gs://calcofi-db/ingest/ncei_dic/

  Files Created

  metadata/ncei/dic/
  ├── tbls_redefine.csv
  └── flds_redefine.csv

  ingest_ncei_dic.qmd              # ingest notebook
  data/wrangling/ncei_dic.duckdb   # wrangling database
  data/parquet/ncei_dic/
  ├── dic_sample.parquet
  ├── dic_measurement.parquet
  ├── measurement_type.parquet
  ├── manifest.json
  └── metadata.json

  Decisions Needed

  1. Replicate handling: Should we average replicate DIC/TA measurements per bottle, or keep them as
  separate rows (e.g., dic_rep1, dic_rep2 like the calcofi.org bottle data)?
  2. Unmatched samples (75%): Should the unmatched DIC samples remain in dic_sample as standalone
  records (with NULL cast_id/bottle_id), or should we attempt broader matching (e.g., by lat/lon
  proximity instead of station key)?
  3. Merge vs. separate: Should dic_measurement rows eventually be merged into the existing
  bottle_measurement table, or stay as a separate table?

  How would you like to proceed with these decisions?
  
1. **Replicate handling**: Should we average replicate DIC/TA measurements per bottle, or keep them as
separate rows (e.g., dic_rep1, dic_rep2 like the calcofi.org bottle data)?

Like `@ingest_calcofi_ctd-cast.qmd`, create a `dic_measurement_summary` table with average and standard deviation (0 if only 1 measurement) for every unique position in time and space (including depth). Update the appropriate skills to reflect this logic for future. Be sure to reference the example code since need to filter out odd values via DuckDB (`NaN,-Inf,Inf`).

2. **Unmatched samples (75%)**: Should the unmatched DIC samples remain in dic_sample as standalone
records (with NULL cast_id/bottle_id), or should we attempt broader matching (e.g., by lat/lon
proximity instead of station key)?

Retain all DIC samples. Create a new high priority issue about mismatches with `site` and spatial matching to `grid` and `segment`. Given `lon+lat`, we can always project to the CalCOFI coordinate system to a `site` (`lon+lat -> line+station`), but there remains questions about what precision to use, and perhaps simply fall back on the common `grid`. (See `@ingest_swfsc_ichthyo.qmd` for example of matching to site and grid.) Ideally, we update sites along a cruise track (a la `segment` table) that gets populated with more observations as datasets get ingested. Then we can add QA/QC checks into the ingest process to identify unrealistic deviations in space and time along the track, corroborated by other datasets.

3. **Merge vs. separate**: Should dic_measurement rows eventually be merged into the existing bottle_measurement table, or stay as a separate table?

Let's keep them seperated for now, but in the notebook and potentially another low-priority issue suggest possibilities for merging.

Let's change this `ingest_ncei_dic.qmd` and Claude skills in a few ways:

**Rename `ingest_ncei_dic.qmd` to `ingest_calcofi_dic.qmd`**. This indicates a provider `calcofi` and dataset `dic`. The provider should not be NCEI since the data is from CalCOFI, even though the dataset is stored on NCEI here: https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/metadata/landing-page/bin/iso?id=gov.noaa.nodc:0301029. Looking there more deeply, we see a **Credit** tab with a suggested Dataset Citation that clearly suggests the provider is CalCOFI:

  Keeling, Charles D.; Lueker, Timothy J.; Emanuele, Guy; Dickson, Andrew G.; Martz, Todd R.; Wolfe, Wiley H.; Mau, Aaron (2025). Discrete profile dissolved inorganic carbon, total alkalinity, water temperature and salinity measurements collected and assembled from a number of cruises on many different ships for the California Cooperative Oceanic Fisheries Investigations program (CalCOFI) in the North East Pacific Ocean Coastal area from 1983-03-19 to 2021-07-20 (NCEI Accession 0301029). [indicate subset used]. NOAA National Centers for Environmental Information. Dataset. https://doi.org/10.25921/3w9f-jd72. Accessed [date].

Other CalCOFI datasets are stored on data portals like EDI or ERDDAP, but should retain a provider indicative of the organization curating it. We should also be storing dataset metadata such as citations (**`citation_main`**, `citation_others` with a semicolon delimited or similar list) and source URLs (**`link_data_source`** and `link_others`), probably in another table **`dataset`**, which could also indicate in a **`tables`** column which tables it is contributing into the integrated database and perhaps whethere wholly or partly. 

The most current information on this dataset is found on the CalCOFI.org website at https://calcofi.org/data/oceanographic-data/dic/. We should include something like **`link_calcofi_org`** to indicate the CalCOFI.org landing page for the dataset. This web page should be scraped for any new or updated data or metadata for the dataset before proceeding with the ingest.

Finally, at the end of each new dataset ingestion script we should be updating the **`release_database.qmd`** "caboose" of ingestion workflows to ensure the dataset gets folded into the integrated database and becomes part of a new release.

Considerations above should be a standard part of the dataset ingestion process, so need to update skills accordingly. Plus, need to revise existing ingest_datasets.

  2. Create the dataset metadata table
  3. Update release_database.qmd
  4. Update skills
  5. Update measurement_type.csv references

Besides updating the `release_database.qmd`, each new ingest dataset script needs to update the **`_targets.R`** file to include the new dataset targets. Revise to apply tidy data principles to the data, per existing datasets. I don't want to measurements for salinity, temp and DIC on the same row. And what measurements are we adding or re-using from the `measurement_type` table?

Update the skills accordingly.

  execute:

⏺ ---
  Summary of Changes

  1. Tidy data structure

  - dic_sample now has only 7 position/time columns: expocode, ship_name, datetime_utc, sta_key,
  latitude, longitude, depth_m — zero measurement values
  - dic_measurement (16,391 rows) pivots ALL 4 measurement types to long format:

  ┌──────────────────┬───────┬────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ measurement_type │   n   │                   status                   │
  ├──────────────────┼───────┼────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ dic              │ 4,237 │ NEW — dissolved inorganic carbon (umol/kg) │
  ├──────────────────┼───────┼────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ alkalinity       │ 4,064 │ NEW — total alkalinity (umol/kg)           │
  ├──────────────────┼───────┼────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ ctdtemp_its90    │ 4,009 │ NEW — CTD temperature ITS-90 (degC)        │
  ├──────────────────┼───────┼────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ salinity_pss78   │ 4,081 │ NEW — practical salinity PSS-78            │
  └──────────────────┴───────┴────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  All 4 are new types distinct from bottle/CTD equivalents (different QC pipeline with WOCE flags).

  - dic_measurement_summary (15,786 rows) — avg/stddev per position

  2. _targets.R updated

  Added ingest_calcofi_dic target depending on ingest_calcofi_bottle, inserted before
  release_database which now depends on all 4 ingests.

  3. Fixed messages (cat vs message)

  Switched all message() → cat(glue(...), "\n"). Messages now render visibly in HTML output.

  4. Fixed preview tables

  Replaced single preview_tables() loop call with individual datatable() calls in separate chunks.
  All 3 tables now render correctly.

  5. Skills updated

  - ingest-new.md: Added tidy data conventions, cat() pattern, individual datatable() calls, updated
  Pattern B description
  - ingest_template.qmd: Switched message→cat
  - Saved feedback memory for future conversations

Help me devise a strategy of developing Claude skills and agents (or even team of agents) for ingesting the rest of these CalCOFI datasets:

- `@~/My Drive/projects/calcofi/docs/CalCOFI - EcoQuants Quote, 2025-10 to 2026-06_v2.docx`
- `@~/My Drive/projects/calcofi/docs/Living program document_ CalCOFI Data Inventory_updated Feb2026.xlsx`

Develop a template by deeply evaluating the following workflows and documentation:

- `@README_PLAN.qmd`
- `@ingest_swfsc_ichthyo.qmd`
- `@ingest_calcofi_bottle.qmd`
- `@ingest_calcofi_ctd-cast.qmd`
- `@release_database.qmd`
- `@publish_ichthyo_to_obis.qmd`
- `@../docs/`

- `@../calcofi4r/`

Also look here `@.` in `workflows` for historical attempts at exploring and ingesting data to create a matrix of resources per dataset and prioritization of approach. Consider creating Github issues and cleaning up existing for developing a path forward.

←  ☐ DIC strategy  ☐ Cephalopods  ☐ Approach  ☐ GH Issues  ✔ Submit  →

**DIC strategy**. For DIC data: should DIC measurements be merged into the existing bottle_measurement table (since DIC
 is sampled from the same Niskin bottles) or kept as a separate ingest with its own tables?

❯ 1. Merge into bottle_measurement (Recommended)
     Add DIC/alkalinity/pH rows to the existing bottle_measurement long-format table via
     measurement_type. Simplest integration since DIC samples correspond to bottle casts. Would
     require updating ingest_calcofi_bottle.qmd or creating a supplemental ingest that appends.
  2. Separate ingest: ingest_calcofi_dic.qmd
     Create a standalone ingest with its own tables (dic_sample, dic_measurement). Keeps DIC
     provenance separate, but adds cross-dataset FK complexity. Better if DIC has different temporal
     coverage or station grid than bottle.
  3. Both -- separate ingest that writes to shared tables
     Create ingest_calcofi_dic.qmd but have it write to the same bottle_measurement table (or a new
     shared measurement table). Separate workflow but unified output.

**Cephalopods**. For cephalopods: are they already in the SWFSC ichthyo database tables (just different species) or do
 they need to be pulled from ERDDAP as a separate source?                                  

  1. Already in SWFSC DB -- just need extraction
     Cephalopod counts/sizes are already in the SWFSC CalCOFI database CSVs (species table includes 
     cephalopod taxa). The existing ichthyo ingest may already capture them, or they need to be
     extracted from the same source.                                                           
  2. Separate source -- need ERDDAP pull    
     Cephalopod data is served separately on ERDDAP (erdCalCOFI datasets) and is not in the SWFSC 
     database CSVs. Needs a new data pull.                                                           
  3. Not sure -- needs investigation      
     I'll investigate the SWFSC species table and ERDDAP to determine the source before planning the 
     ingest approach.                                                                               
❯ 4. Some of these datasets, like `Datataset name` = "Invertebrate count (Cephalopods)", `Where are data dynamically served?`="https://coastwatch.pfeg.noaa.gov/erddap/tabledap/erdCalCOFIinvcnt.html", are a published dataset package that is a subset of the full dataset, in this case the ceaphalopods from `swfsc_ichthyo`, that is published to erddap. So a workflow `publish_icthyo-cephalopods_to_erddap.qmd` (like `publish_icthyo_to_obis.qmd`) is to be created to feed this endpoint, but we do not need (or want) another `ingest_*.qmd` workflow.

**Approach**. Should I implement all 5 Claude skills in Phase 0, or start with just the first dataset (DIC)        
end-to-end and extract the skill patterns from that experience?                            

  1. Build skills first, then use them          
     Create all 5 skills upfront in Phase 0 (1 week). More structured but skills may need revision  
     once they hit real data.                                                                   
  2. DIC first, then extract skills (Recommended)                                              
     Do DIC end-to-end manually (using existing patterns), then extract/generalize the workflow into 
     reusable skills. The skills will be battle-tested from real experience.                      
❯ 3. Parallel -- skills + DIC simultaneously                                                         
     Build skills while also working on DIC. Use DIC as the test case to validate skills as they're 
     built.

**GH Issues**. For GitHub issues: should I create them now as part of this planning session, or defer to            
implementation?                                                                            

❯ 1. Create issues now                          
     Create all new issues and close/consolidate old ones during this session. Gives immediate      
     visibility to the full roadmap.                                                            
  2. Create issues during implementation                                                       
     Defer issue creation to when we start each phase. Avoids premature issue creation for datasets  
     that may change scope.                                                                       
  3. Create must-complete issues now, defer rest                                                     
     Create issues for the 5 must-complete datasets and 2 must-complete publishes now. Create       
     should/nice-to-have issues later.

 Strategy: Claude Skills & Agents for CalCOFI Dataset Ingestion

 Context

 The CalCOFI contract (Oct 2025 - Jun 2026, $44K) requires ingesting additional datasets and
 publishing many to portals (OBIS, ERDDAP, EDI). Three production ingests are complete (ichthyo,
 bottle, ctd-cast), plus a release workflow and OBIS publish workflow. The existing pipeline is
 well-architected with 120+ reusable calcofi4db functions. The goal is to create Claude Code skills
 that systematize the remaining work, while building the DIC ingest in parallel as a test case.

 Key insight: Many "datasets" in the CalCOFI inventory are actually publish workflows from
 already-ingested data (e.g., cephalopods are a subset of ichthyo published to ERDDAP), not new
 ingest workflows. The matrix below separates true ingests from publish-only workflows.

 ---
 Claude Code Skill Architecture

 Skill 1: /explore-dataset

 Purpose: Profile a new CSV or ERDDAP dataset before ingestion.

 Implementation: R script that:
 1. Reads CSV into DuckDB in-memory, runs SUMMARIZE on each table
 2. Checks for columns matching existing PKs (cruise_key, site_uuid, cast_id, etc.)
 3. Profiles lat/lon against CalCOFI grid extent
 4. Profiles date ranges, identifies temporal overlap with existing cruises
 5. Checks species columns against existing species table
 6. Outputs structured markdown report

 Location: .claude/skills/explore-dataset.md + scripts/explore_dataset.R

 Skill 2: /generate-metadata

 Purpose: Create metadata/{provider}/{dataset}/ with scaffolded tbls_redefine.csv and
 flds_redefine.csv by calling calcofi4db functions.

 Implementation:
 1. Creates directory structure
 2. Calls read_csv_metadata() to inventory source CSVs
 3. Calls create_redefinition_files() to scaffold redefine CSVs
 4. Prints instructions for manual column renaming decisions

 Location: .claude/skills/generate-metadata.md

 Skill 3: /ingest-new

 Purpose: Scaffold a new ingest_{provider}_{dataset}.qmd from parameterized template.

 Template structure (from the 3 production workflows):
 UNIVERSAL (~200 lines): YAML, setup, read_csv_files(), check_data_integrity(), ingest_dataset()
 SEMI-CUSTOM (~150 lines): load_prior_tables(), add_point_geom(), assign_grid_key(),
 write_parquet_outputs(), sync_to_gcs()
 DATASET-SPECIFIC (~500+ lines): PK strategy, table consolidation, measurement pivoting, taxonomy,
 validation

 Parameters: provider, dataset, dataset_name, source_url, has_taxonomy, has_long_pivot,
 spatial_table, lon_col, lat_col

 Location: .claude/skills/ingest-new.md + .claude/skills/templates/ingest_template.qmd

 Skill 4: /validate-ingest

 Purpose: Post-ingest validation on a wrangling DuckDB.

 Checks: PK uniqueness, FK integrity, NULL rates, coordinate ranges, date ranges, row count sanity,
 orphan records.

 Location: .claude/skills/validate-ingest.md

 Skill 5: /publish-template

 Purpose: Scaffold a publish workflow for a target portal (OBIS, ERDDAP, EDI).

 Sub-templates:
 - OBIS: Event Core + Occurrence + eMoF (following publish_ichthyo_to_obis.qmd)
 - ERDDAP: NCCSV metadata generation for datasets.xml submission
 - EDI: EML metadata + data package generation

 Location: .claude/skills/publish-template.md +
 .claude/skills/templates/publish_{portal}_template.qmd

 Agent Strategy: Skill-Chained Workflow

 For each new dataset, chain skills with human-in-the-loop:

 /explore-dataset {csv_path}       --> human reviews profile report
 /generate-metadata {provider} {dataset}  --> human edits flds_redefine.csv
 /ingest-new {provider} {dataset}         --> human fills dataset-specific sections
 /validate-ingest {provider} {dataset}    --> human reviews flagged rows
 /publish-template {provider} {dataset} {portal}  --> human fills portal metadata

 Human stays in the loop because each dataset has unique domain knowledge requirements, metadata
 redefinitions need scientific judgment, and domain experts validate outputs.

 Infrastructure issues:

 Issue template (each ingest issue):
 ```md
 ## Dataset
 - **Name**: {name}
 - **Provider**: {provider}
 - **Contact**: {person}
 - **Source**: {URL or path}
 - **Coverage**: {temporal range}

 ## Checklist
 - [ ] Data available on Google Drive
 - [ ] `/explore-dataset` profile completed
 - [ ] `/generate-metadata` redefinition files created
 - [ ] `flds_redefine.csv` reviewed by domain expert
 - [ ] `/ingest-new` workflow scaffolded
 - [ ] Dataset-specific transforms implemented
 - [ ] `/validate-ingest` passes
 - [ ] Cross-dataset FKs validated
 - [ ] Parquet exported & uploaded to GCS
 - [ ] `_targets.R` updated
 - [ ] `release_database.qmd` updated
 - [ ] PR reviewed & merged

 ## Dependencies
 - Depends on: {prior ingests}
 - Needed by: {publish workflows}
 ```

 ---
 Phased Roadmap

 Phase 0+1 (Parallel): Infrastructure + DIC (Weeks 1-3, Mar 17 - Apr 4)

 Build skills while simultaneously doing DIC end-to-end. DIC validates the skill patterns.

 - Create 5 Claude Code skills in .claude/skills/
 - Create ingest/publish templates
 - Create all GitHub issues + close stale ones
 - DIC ingest (2-3 days) -- merge into bottle_measurement, extend bottle ingest or create
 supplemental
 - Refine skills based on DIC experience

 Phase 2: Remaining Must-Complete Ingests (Weeks 4-6, Apr 7 - Apr 25)

 - Euphausiids ingest (3-5 days) -- single CSV, species counts by net tow
 - Zooplankton biovolume ingest (5-7 days) -- PIC data, station matching

 Phase 3: Must-Complete Publishes + Complex Ingest (Weeks 7-10, Apr 28 - May 23)

 - Phytoplankton ingest (7-10 days) -- most complex, multiple sources
 - CTD to ERDDAP publish (3-5 days)
 - Bottle to ERDDAP publish (3-5 days)
 - Ichthyo subsets to ERDDAP publishes: cephalopods, larvae, eggs (2-3 days each)

 Phase 4: Should-Complete (Weeks 11-13, May 26 - Jun 13)

 - Seabird + Marine mammals ingests + OBIS publishes
 - ZooDb ingest
 - DIC to ERDDAP, Zooplankton to EDI publishes

 Phase 5: Nice-to-Have (Weeks 14+, Jun 16-30)

 - PRODO, CUFES, ZooScan, lobster phyllosoma -- only if time permits

 ---
 Key Architectural Decisions

 A. DIC → bottle_measurement (Confirmed)

 DIC measurements merge into existing bottle_measurement table since DIC is sampled from the same
 Niskin bottles. Add dic, alkalinity, ph entries to measurement_type.csv. Map via cast_id or
 cruise+station+depth matching to existing casts table.

 B. Cephalopods = Publish, Not Ingest (Confirmed)

 Cephalopods are already in the ichthyo ingest (subset of species in the SWFSC database). Create
 publish_ichthyo-cephalopods_to_erddap.qmd to extract and format for ERDDAP. Same pattern applies to
  larvae counts/sizes/stages and egg counts/stages -- all publish workflows from existing ichthyo
 data.

 C. Table Naming

 New ingest tables follow {dataset}_{table} pattern. Shared tables (ship, cruise, grid,
 measurement_type, species, taxon) loaded from ichthyo via load_prior_tables().

 D. Cross-Dataset Matching

 Standard: derive_cruise_key_on_casts() by ship_key + datetime. For datasets without ship/cruise
 IDs: line+station+date proximity.

 E. measurement_type Unification

 metadata/measurement_type.csv (100 entries) is the reference. New datasets add entries before
 implementing pivot logic.

 F. release_database.qmd

 Each new ingest adds a parquet_dirs entry. Release "caboose" assembles all and validates
 cross-dataset integrity.

 ---

 ├─────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 Verification

 After Phase 0+1 (skills + DIC):
 1. Test each skill on the DIC dataset
 2. Verify DIC measurements appear in bottle_measurement table
 3. Verify cross-dataset FK integrity with existing casts
 4. Run release_database.qmd with DIC included

 After each subsequent ingest:
 1. Render ingest QMD end-to-end
 2. /validate-ingest passes
 3. release_database.qmd includes new dataset
 4. Cross-dataset FK integrity verified
 5. Parquet files on GCS

## 2026-03-17.b `2601RL` not processed by `@ingest_calcofi_ctd-cast.qmd`

Why did `2601RL` not get processed in `@ingest_calcofi_ctd-cast.qmd` between `## Scrape CTD Download Links` and `## Find and Prioritize CTD Data Files`.

⏺ Found the issue. Compare the directory structures:                        
                                                                               
  - 2501RL (works): has a db-csvs subdirectory → path matches                  
  "Prelim.*db[_|-]csv"                                                         
  - 2401RL: has a nested subdirectory 20-2401RL_CTDPrelim/ (likely contains    
  db-csvs inside)                                                              
  - 2601RL (broken): CSV files are directly in the top-level directory — no    
  db-csvs or db_csv subfolder                                                  
                                                                               
  The data_stage classification at line 246-257 requires the path to match     
  "Prelim.*db[_|-]csv" for preliminary data. Since 2601RL's path is            
  20-2601RL_CTDPrelim/20-2601_CTD_001-116U.csv (no db-csv segment), data_stage 
  is set to NA, and line 270 filters it out:                                   

  filter(data_stage %in% c("final", "preliminary"))
                                                                               
  This is the same issue that already required special-case handling for 2507SR
   and 2111SR. The fix is to add 2601RL to the special cases, or better yet,   
  generalize the pattern. Would you like me to make the data_stage logic more  
  robust — e.g., fall back to matching just "Prelim.*\\.csv$" for any cruise
  where the CSVs aren't in a db-csv subdirectory?

⏺ Now I see the full picture. All three cases have the same pattern — CSVs     
  directly in the Prelim directory with no db-csvs subfolder:                  
                                                                               
  - 2507SR: 20-2507SR_CTDPrelim/20-2507_CTD_*.csv (flat)                       
  - 2601RL: 20-2601RL_CTDPrelim/20-2601_CTD_*.csv (flat)                       
  - 2111SR: unique — CSVs only in csvs-plots subfolder                         
                                                                               
  I'll generalize the flat-Prelim case and keep 2111SR as its only true edge   
  case.                                      

⏺ The fix replaces the cruise-specific 2507SR special case with a general rule:

  str_detect(dir_unzip, "Prelim") &                                            
    path_unzip == paste0(dir_unzip, "/", file_csv) ~ "preliminary",            
                                                                               
  This matches any CSV sitting directly in a Prelim directory (no intermediate 
  subdirectory), which handles 2601RL, 2507SR, and any future cruises with the
  same flat layout. It won't accidentally pull in files from csvs-plots,       
  metadata, or asc-hdr-btl subdirectories for cruises that have a proper
  db-csvs folder.

  The 2111SR edge case is kept since its CSVs genuinely live in a different    
  subdirectory (csvs-plots).
                                                                               
  Also removed the stale # NOT FOUND comment for 2601RL since the URL is now   
  available.

## 2026-03-17 ctd-viz shiny app
(cd apps)

Now that the `@../workflows/ingest_calcofi_ctd-cast.qmd` has ingested the large `ctd-cast` dataset with 15 GB of `ctd_measurement` (partitioned by `cruise_key`), let's use the the client-side R functions @../calcofi4r (eg, starting with `calcofi4r::cc_get_db()`) to make a new app `ctd-viz` to:

1. Visualize a **map** of one cruise at a time with segments. \
   (Might need to create segments for this dataset. See previous segment creation with `ichthyo` dataset. This would be best done at dataset ingestion in `@../workflows/ingest_calcofi_ctd-cast.qmd` if not already there.)
2. Visualize a **transect** from selected cruise segments with a start and end datetime.
  - Ideally, begin/end points on the map could be selected spatially, or temporally on a timeline from begin to end of cruise.
  - Explore these options for visualizing the transect:
    * best: [**odv\_bathy**](https://theoceancode.netlify.app/post/odv_bathy/)
    * [PlotSvalbard::section\_plot()](https://mikkovihtakari.github.io/PlotSvalbard/reference/section_plot.html)  
      ggplot() \-\> interactive: plotly::ggplotly(), [ggiraph](https://davidgohel.github.io/ggiraph/)
    * [ggOceanPlots](https://github.com/MikkoVihtakari/ggOceanPlots)
    * [MBA::mba.surf()](https://www.rdocumentation.org/packages/MBA/versions/0.0-8/topics/mba.surf)
    * [R oceanographic transect section plot \- Google Search](https://www.google.com/search?q=R+oceanographic+transect+section+plot)
  - Ideally, we will supplement with best available bathymetric data to provide the seafloor in the transect visual. (Might need to filter/flag/correct CTD samples occurring below seafloor.)
  - Ideally, this visual could be interactive to interrogate values and a maximum distance in space and time for interpolation set as a preference.

←  ☐ DB connection  ☐ Selection UX  ☐ Segments  ✔ Submit  →
                                                                               
**DB connection**. The CTD data lives in a separate DuckDB file (calcofi-ctd.duckdb) from the 
ichthyo data (calcofi.duckdb). The prototype connects directly to this file.   
Should we use the httpfs remote-attach pattern (like querychat) pointing at the
 CTD-specific DuckDB, or is the CTD data already available via
calcofi4r::cc_get_db()?

  1. httpfs to calcofi-ctd.duckdb (Recommended)
     Same pattern as querychat app: ATTACH remote DuckDB via httpfs. Works now
     with the existing CTD file at file.calcofi.io.
  2. calcofi4r::cc_get_db()
     Use the frozen parquet release system. Only works if CTD data is already
     in a released version.
  3. Local DuckDB file path
     Like the prototype: direct file path with Darwin/Linux switch for ~/My
     Drive/... vs /share/public/data
❯ 4. Disregard the `querychat` app, which has patterns I want to skip, including use of local calcofi-ctd.duckdb. Use `calcofi4r::cc_get_db()` and think freshly about these tasks (do not repeat querychat app patterns).

**Selection UX**. For the transect begin/end selection, which interaction model do you prefer?
                                                                               
  1. Click two map markers (Recommended)                                       
     Click cast point markers on the map to set begin and end. Simple, works 
     with leaflet click events. All casts between them (by temporal/ordinal 
     order) form the transect.
  2. Timeline slider below map                 
     A datetime range slider below the map to select temporal begin/end. More  
     visual but more complex to implement.         
  3. Both options          
     Support both click-on-map and timeline slider, synced together. Most     
     flexible but most complex.
❯ 4. Use both options to select begin/end either spatially or temporally, but also use the mapgl library for mapping like `@../int-app`, which has many preferred patterns.

**Segments**. Should the segment creation also be added to the ingest workflow            
(ingest_calcofi_ctd-cast.qmd) as part of this task, or just computed on-the-fly
 in the app for now?                                                           
                                                                             
❯ 1. App-only for now (Recommended)                                         
     Compute segments on-the-fly from ctd_cast data in the app. Fast enough for
      a single cruise (~100-500 casts). Add to ingest workflow later.
  2. Both app and ingest workflow                                              
     Also add a ctd_segment table to the ingest workflow, following the ichthyo
      segment pattern. Requires re-running the ingest.

⏺ The plan is updated. Key changes from your feedback:  
                                                                               
  - calcofi4r::cc_get_db() for database connection (no httpfs/querychat        
  patterns)                                                                    
  - mapgl::maplibre() for mapping (not leaflet), following int-app patterns    
  - Both map click + timeline slider for transect begin/end selection, synced  
  together                                                                     
  - bslib::page_sidebar() with navset_card_underline (int-app layout pattern)  
  - Segments computed on-the-fly in the app only                               
                                                                               

 Ready to code?                                                                
                                                                               
 Here is Claude's plan:

 Plan: ctd-viz Shiny App                                

 Context

 CalCOFI's CTD cast dataset (~15 GB of ctd_measurement partitioned by
 cruise_key) has been ingested via workflows/ingest_calcofi_ctd-cast.qmd. The
 data lives in DuckDB tables: ctd_cast (cast-level metadata with geometry),
 ctd_measurement (long-format sensor readings at depth), ctd_summary
 (pre-aggregated stats), and measurement_type (reference). No segments exist
 for CTD data yet — computed on-the-fly in the app.

 An early prototype exists at apps_dev/ctd-viz/app.R. The new app will be
 production-quality at apps/ctd-viz/ using calcofi4r::cc_get_db() and patterns
  from the int-app (mapgl, bslib page_sidebar, etc.).

 File Structure

 apps/ctd-viz/
 ├── global.R    -- packages, cc_get_db() connection, startup queries, helper
 functions
 ├── ui.R        -- bslib page_sidebar with 2 tabs (Cruise Map, Transect)
 └── server.R    -- reactive logic: map interaction, transect plotting

 ---
 Step 1: global.R — Setup & Helpers

 Packages (following int-app pattern)

 librarian::shelf(
   bslib, bsicons, calcofi/calcofi4r,
   DBI, dbplyr, dplyr, duckdb,
   geosphere, ggplot2, glue,
   lubridate, mapgl, marmap, MBA,
   plotly, purrr, sf, shiny,
   tidyr, viridis,
   quiet = T)

 Database connection

 con <- calcofi4r::cc_get_db()
 dbExecute(con, "INSTALL spatial; LOAD spatial;")

 Startup queries (lightweight, run once)

 1. cruise_choices: distinct cruise_key from ctd_cast, joined with
 cruise.date_ym and ship.ship_name → named vector for selectInput (ordered
 desc by cruise_key for most recent first)
 2. meas_types: full measurement_type table filtered to CTD types → for
 variable selector (show description (units))
 3. default cruise: pick most recent cruise_key as initial selection

 Helper functions

 compute_segments(casts_df) — On-the-fly segment creation (ichthyo pattern
 from ingest_swfsc_ichthyo.qmd:806-890):
 - Input: data.frame with ctd_cast_uuid, datetime_utc, lat_dec, lon_dec,
 ord_occ, sta_key, line, sta
 - Sort by ord_occ then datetime_utc
 - Lag to get begin/end points for consecutive casts
 - Create sf::st_linestring() geometries between consecutive casts
 - Compute length_km via sf::st_length()
 - Return sf data.frame of segment lines

 get_transect_bathy(transect_pts) — Bathymetry along transect:
 - marmap::getNOAA.bathy() with keep = TRUE for disk caching
 - marmap::get.depth() at interpolated points along the transect path
 - Return tibble with dist_km, bathy_depth_m

 build_transect_plot(meas_data, bathy_data, meas_label, max_depth, interp_n) —
  ODV-style plot:
 - MBA::mba.surf() for 2D interpolation (dist_km × depth_m → value)
 - ggplot: geom_raster() + geom_contour() + bathymetry polygon + station rug
 marks
 - Return ggplot object (caller wraps with ggplotly())

 ---
 Step 2: ui.R — Layout (following int-app's bslib patterns)

 Use bslib::page_sidebar() with navset_card_underline for tabs (like int-app),
  not page_navbar.

 Sidebar (persistent across tabs)

 - selectInput("sel_cruise") — single cruise selection
 - actionButton("btn_load_cruise") — "Load Cruise"
 - hr()
 - Transect selection info — htmlOutput("txt_selection_info") showing
 begin/end stations
 - actionButton("btn_reset_selection") — "Reset Selection"
 - hr()
 - Transect plot controls (visible when Transect tab active):
   - selectInput("sel_meas_type") — measurement variable
   - selectInput("sel_cast_dir") — "Downcast (D)" / "Upcast (U)", default "D"
   - sliderInput("sl_max_depth") — 0–5000, default 500
   - numericInput("num_interp_n") — MBA grid resolution, default 200
   - checkboxInput("chk_bathy") — "Show bathymetry", default TRUE
   - actionButton("btn_plot") — "Plot Transect"

 Main content — navset_card_underline

 - "Cruise Map" tab: maplibreOutput("map_cruise", height = "100%")
 - "Transect" tab: plotlyOutput("plot_transect", height = "100%")

 Below map: Timeline slider

 - sliderInput("sl_time_range") — datetime range slider spanning the cruise's
 begin/end datetimes
 - Synced with map click selection: clicking cast markers updates the slider,
 dragging slider updates map highlights

 ---
 Step 3: server.R — Reactive Logic

 Reactive values

 rv <- reactiveValues(
   cruise_casts    = NULL,   # sf: all casts for selected cruise
   cruise_segments = NULL,   # sf: computed segment lines
   sel_begin_idx   = NULL,   # integer index (by ord_occ order) of begin cast
   sel_end_idx     = NULL,   # integer index of end cast
   transect_casts  = NULL    # subset of casts between begin/end
 )

 Flow 1: Load Cruise → Map

 observeEvent(input$btn_load_cruise):
 1. Query ctd_cast WHERE cruise_key = sel_cruise, collect
 2. Convert to sf with st_as_sf(coords = c("lon_dec", "lat_dec"), crs = 4326)
 3. Call compute_segments() to create segment lines
 4. Store in rv$cruise_casts, rv$cruise_segments
 5. Update timeline slider range to cruise's min/max datetime
 6. Reset selection state

 Map rendering with mapgl::maplibre() (following int-app patterns):
 - Base style: carto_style("voyager") (light mode)
 - Cast points as circle layer via add_circle_layer() colored by line number
 - Segment lines via add_line_layer()
 - Navigation and scale controls
 - fit_bounds() to cruise extent

 Flow 2: Transect Selection (dual: map click + timeline slider)

 Map click — observeEvent(input$map_cruise_feature_click):
 1. First click → set rv$sel_begin_idx, add green highlight marker via
 maplibre_proxy()
 2. Second click → set rv$sel_end_idx, add red highlight marker
 3. Subset casts between begin/end by ord_occ order → rv$transect_casts
 4. Highlight selected segments (thicker/brighter line) via proxy
 set_paint_property()
 5. Update timeline slider to match selected time range

 Timeline slider — observeEvent(input$sl_time_range):
 1. Filter rv$cruise_casts to casts within slider's datetime range
 2. Set rv$sel_begin_idx and rv$sel_end_idx from first/last filtered cast
 3. Update rv$transect_casts
 4. Update map highlights via proxy

 Both selection methods stay synced — changing one updates the other.

 Flow 3: Plot Transect

 eventReactive(input$btn_plot):
 1. Query measurements (performance-critical):
 SELECT m.depth_m, m.measurement_value,
        c.sta_key, c.lat_dec, c.lon_dec, c.ord_occ
 FROM ctd_measurement m
 INNER JOIN ctd_cast c ON m.ctd_cast_uuid = c.ctd_cast_uuid
 WHERE m.cruise_key = '{cruise_key}'           -- partition pruning!
   AND m.measurement_type = '{meas_type}'
   AND c.cast_dir = '{cast_dir}'
   AND c.ctd_cast_uuid IN ({uuid_list})
 ORDER BY c.ord_occ, m.depth_m
 1. Single cruise × single variable × selected casts → typically 10K-100K
 rows.
 2. Compute distance: cumulative haversine distance via
 geosphere::distHaversine() between consecutive cast locations
 3. Interpolate: MBA::mba.surf(data.frame(dist_km, depth_m, value), no.X = n,
 no.Y = n, extend = TRUE)
 4. Fetch bathymetry (if chk_bathy): get_transect_bathy() along the transect
 path, cached
 5. Build plot: build_transect_plot() → plotly::ggplotly() for interactivity

 ---
 Step 4: ODV-Style Transect Plot Details

 ggplot construction

 ggplot() +
   geom_raster(data = d_grid, aes(dist_km, depth_m, fill = value), interpolate
  = TRUE) +
   scale_fill_viridis_c(name = meas_label) +
   geom_contour(data = d_grid, aes(dist_km, depth_m, z = value),
                color = "white", alpha = 0.4, linewidth = 0.3) +
   geom_rug(data = station_pos, aes(x = dist_km), sides = "t") +
   geom_polygon(data = bathy_polygon, aes(dist_km, depth_m),
                fill = "gray40", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
   scale_y_reverse(limits = c(max_depth, 0), expand = c(0, 0)) +
   scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
   labs(x = "Distance (km)", y = "Depth (m)",
        title = glue("{meas_desc} — Cruise {cruise_key}")) +
   theme_minimal() +
   theme(panel.grid = element_blank())

  Created: apps/ctd-viz/                                                       
                                                                  
  3 files following the int-app patterns (bslib page_sidebar, mapgl,           
  cc_get_db()):                                                   
                                                                               
  global.R                                                        

  - Connects via calcofi4r::cc_get_db() (skips LOAD spatial which breaks       
  dbplyr)
  - Builds cruise choices from casts table joined with cruise + ship (521      
  cruises, 1949-1999)                                                          
  - Loads measurement_type reference (100 types; defaults to temperature)
  - Helper functions: compute_segments(), get_transect_bathy(),                
  build_transect_plot()                                                        
                                                                               
  ui.R                                                                         
                                                                  
  - page_sidebar with navset_card_underline (2 tabs: Cruise Map, Transect)     
  - Sidebar: cruise selector, load button, selection info, reset, transect plot
   controls                                                                    
  - Cruise Map tab: maplibreOutput + time range slider            
  - Transect tab: plotlyOutput                                                 
                                                                  
  server.R                                                                     
                                                                  
  - Flow 1 — Load Cruise: queries casts, creates sf points + segment lines,    
  renders maplibre map with base + highlight layers
  - Flow 2 — Select Transect: click two casts on map OR drag time slider;      
  bidirectional sync with circular-update guard; map highlights via            
  set_filter/set_paint_property proxy
  - Flow 3 — Plot Transect: queries bottle + bottle_measurement, computes      
  cumulative haversine distance, MBA interpolation, optional marmap bathymetry,
   ggplot ODV-style plot → ggplotly
                                                                               
  Key adaptation from plan                                                     
  
  The ctd_measurement table (234M rows, partitioned) exists in the catalog but 
  its parquet files haven't been uploaded to the GCS release yet. The app uses
  the available casts + bottle + bottle_measurement tables instead. When       
  ctd_measurement is released, switching back requires changing the table names
   and join path in global.R/server.R.

  Key changes in this update:                                                  
                                                                  
  global.R:                                                                    
  - Uses ctd_cast for cruise choices (93 CTD cruises)

  display

⏺ All files pass syntax. The key performance fixes:

  - Cruise list: reads partition directory names from parquet_scan(...,        
  hive_partitioning=true) (~6s) instead of scanning 6M ctd_cast rows for
  DISTINCT cruise_key                                                          
  - Measurement types: scans a single cruise partition (~1s) instead of all
  234M rows                                                                    
  - No _source_table: uses actual partition data, not provenance metadata

⏺ Two issues here:

  1. `_source_table` is fine — you're right, it works well for filtering CTD
  types. I'll switch back to it.
  2. `tbl(con, "ctd_measurement")` fails because `cc_get_db()` creates the view
  with `s3://` URLs but doesn't configure the httpfs S3 settings. My app's
  `global.R` already configures httpfs after `cc_get_db()`, so the app should work.
   But the RStudio console doesn't have those settings.

  Let me update `global.R` to use `_source_table` for measurement types (instant,
  no remote scan) and keep the httpfs setup for everything else.

⏺ Syntax OK. Summary of the startup query strategy:

  - Measurement types: `_source_table` == "ctd_raw" from the small
  measurement_type reference table (instant, ~100 rows)
  - Cruise list: `parquet_scan(..., hive_partitioning=true)` reads partition
  directory names (~6s, no data scan)
  - httpfs S3 settings: always configured after `cc_get_db()` — this is the key
  fix for the error you're seeing. `cc_get_db()` creates the `ctd_measurement` view
   with `s3://` URLs, but doesn't persist the httpfs settings. The app re-applies
   them on every startup.

      14 +# ensure partitioned tables are available; cc_get_db() glob ov
